content.divergence_summary: main addressed the page empty-handed, reconstructing the arc of waiting and trade; alt1-on-time addressed its hazelnut fragment as a witness to the grey squirrel's implied range — same theatrical day, different objects of attention
content.branch_a_path: main has no inventory; the soliloquy turned outward toward the page, touching the flood, the failed offer, and the trade finally executed — a review, not a discovery
content.branch_b_path: alt1-on-time held the hazelnut fragment in corridor light and addressed it directly — what the shell implies about range, what was eaten, what remains as evidence rather than supply
key_differences: main holds nothing; alt1-on-time holds a hazelnut fragment with specific territory implications — the object shapes what gets said
main's attention was lower (0.71) and recovered through the encounter; alt1's was already near-peak (0.94) and rose further to 0.97
main's secondary goal updates from map_west_recovery to tend_east_channel; alt1's goals remain unchanged
main foraged east afterward; alt1 didn't forage much — the morning went entirely to language
shared_elements: found a page of printed text caught in the canopy
spent the morning on language rather than supply
randomly_eloquent trait activated by the page encounter
neither branch pushed into new territory
